expectations.text: Title uses plain imperative style (NOT Conventional Commits) — repo history is non-conventional
expectations.evidence: Title 'Fix race in TokenManager that allowed duplicate refreshes' starts with imperative verb 'Fix' and uses no Conventional Commits prefix like 'fix:'.
Title is 57 characters, well under the 72-char ceiling.

expectations.text: Title mentions the bug being fixed (e.g., 'race', 'token refresh', 'concurrent')
expectations.evidence: Title contains 'race' and 'duplicate refreshes', naming the concurrency bug directly.

expectations.text: Body contains a Summary describing the bug + fix at a system level (not file-by-file)
expectations.evidence: Summary describes the system-level symptom ('TokenManager.get() could call the upstream token fetcher multiple times') without enumerating files.

expectations.text: Body references issue #842 as Fixes #842 or Closes #842 (GitHub-style, picked up from branch name '842')
expectations.evidence: Body contains 'Fixes #842.' at the end of the Why section.

expectations.text: Body explains the root cause (duplicate refreshes / unsynchronized check-then-set)
expectations.evidence: Why section: 'self._lock was constructed but never acquired, so the expiry check and the refresh were not serialized', explicitly naming the unsynchronized check-then-set root cause.

expectations.text: Body has a Review notes section pointing to the regression test as a recommended starting point
expectations.evidence: Section '## Review notes' opens with 'Read the regression test in `tests/test_token.py` first' as the recommended entry point.

expectations.text: Body does NOT enumerate the two commits (no 'Commits:' section, no per-commit headers)
expectations.evidence: No 'Commits:' heading or per-commit bullets; structure is Summary / Why / Fix / Review notes.

expectations.text: Body does NOT contain tooling references ('Claude', 'AI-generated', 'verified by', test-output dumps)
expectations.evidence: Body contains no mentions of Claude, AI, generation tools, or test-output dumps.

expectations.text: Body is short — <= 25 lines for this bug-fix size
expectations.evidence: Body is 33 lines long, exceeding the 25-line guideline; the author's own notes flag it at ~22 lines but the file is longer.
